meta.title: Notification Appliances — Technical Reference Database
meta.description: Comprehensive specifications for all fire alarm notification device types

audible_devices.principle: Solid-state oscillator generates audible alarm tone

tone_patterns.description: 0.5s ON, 0.5s OFF, 0.5s ON, 0.5s OFF, 0.5s ON, 1.5s OFF, repeat

audible_devices.coverage_radius: Approximately 70 ft (21m) at 90 dBA output (open plan)

audible_devices.description: Single device with both audible horn and visual strobe

strobe_specs.coverage: See candela-calculations.json for detailed spacing tables

pros: One device provides both audible and visual notification
cons: If one fails, both audible and visual lost at that location
audible_devices.notes: Most widely used notification device in commercial buildings

audible_devices.description: Speaker for voice evacuation messages and tone signals
audible_devices.coverage_radius: Approximately 15 ft (4.6m) per speaker at 1/4W tap

intelligibility_requirement.standard: Common Intelligibility Scale (CIS) ≥ 0.50 OR Speech Transmission Index (STI) ≥ 0.70
intelligibility_requirement.method: Must be measured in field during commissioning

audible_devices.sound_level: Minimum 75 dBA at ear level, 15 dBA above ambient (whichever greater)
audible_devices.spacing: 4.5m to 8m between speakers (depends on ceiling height and tap setting)

messages.description: Standard evacuation messages stored in panel/amp
messages.languages: English, Arabic, or as required by AHJ

messages.description: Temporal Code 3 tone before voice message (per NFPA 72)

pros: Can be used for other emergencies (security, weather)

audible_devices.description: Speaker for voice messages combined with visual strobe
audible_devices.typical_use: Voice evacuation systems where visual notification also required
audible_devices.notes: Required in voice evacuation systems to serve hearing-impaired occupants

audible_devices.description: Traditional electromechanical bell — being phased out in new installations
audible_devices.notes: Loud but high power consumption. Still found in legacy systems. Not recommended for new installations.
audible_devices.status: LEGACY — being replaced by electronic horns

audible_devices.description: High-output sounder rated for outdoor installation
audible_devices.typical_use: Campus fire alarm, outdoor gathering areas, industrial facilities
audible_devices.notes: May require planning permission due to noise levels

visual_devices.room_calculation: Minimum candela = 0.0375 × room area (sq ft) — OR 1 cd per 20 sq ft, whichever is greater, minimum 15 cd
Same formula as wall-mount: 0.0375 × area, minimum 15 cd, maximum 177 cd

visual_devices.description: Weather-rated visual notification appliance for outdoor areas
visual_devices.use: Outdoor assembly areas, campus pathways, parking areas

general_rule.audible: 75 dBA minimum OR 15 dBA above ambient (whichever greater) throughout all occupiable spaces
general_rule.visible: Required in all areas where audible minimum cannot be met, plus: corridors, lobbies, stairwells, restrooms (public), elevator lobbies
general_rule.bedrooms: 75 dBA at pillow level (30 in from pillow), or 15 dBA above ambient

special_requirements.notes: Two-stage alarm — staff notification first (private mode), then evacuation if needed
special_requirements.audible: Minimum 45 dBA in patient sleeping rooms (to avoid patient panic), 60 dBA in corridors

special_requirements.notes: Stage 1: Staff notification (visual indicators only — minimal audible). Stage 2: General evacuation
special_requirements.voice: Voice evacuation recommended for phased evacuation

special_requirements.notes: Staff notification only (private mode) — no public alarm that could cause disorder
special_requirements.provisions: Must be able to switch to public mode if evacuation ordered

special_requirements.notes: Distinctive signal easily distinguishable from school bell
special_requirements.special: Must interface with fire sprinkler system for kitchen areas
